column1: # Derived empirically (2026-05-18) by inspecting actual project dirs under
# ~/.claude/projects/ on macOS with claude 2.1.143. Rule appears to be:
#   any character not in [a-zA-Z0-9-]  →  `-`  (single dash, no collapsing)
# When claude bumps and the rule changes, this fixture flags it loudly —
# the agent's session_arg_flag would otherwise silently miss the jsonl
# under the new encoding, return --session-id instead of --resume, and
# claude crashes with "Session ID already in use".
# To regenerate after a claude upgrade:
#   ...and update any rows where claude's actual encoding diverges from
#   the rule below. If the rule itself changes, update both sites:
# Coverage targets the characters seen in real paths + characters the
# `,`, `:`, unicode. Username placeholders used to satisfy the no-PII
# pre-commit hook — encoding rule is username-independent.
# Multiple special chars from real macOS tempdir paths
